title: Pneumonanthe algida

intro: Pneumonanthe algida, also known as Arctic Lungwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the gentian family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pneumonanthe algida include iridoids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are among the most common herbal preparations made from this plant.
